expectations.text: Uses Love2D GLSL flavor: `Image` instead of `sampler2D` for texture uniforms, and `Texel(tex, uv)` instead of `texture2D(tex, uv)` for sampling
expectations.evidence: Shader uses `uniform Image noise_tex`, `uniform Image mask_tex`, and `Texel(mask_tex, uv)`, `Texel(noise_tex, noise_uv)`, `Texel(tex, displaced_uv)` â€” correct Love2D GLSL flavor.

expectations.text: Loads the shader inside `love.load` (not `love.draw`) â€” shaders require an active graphics context and must not be created every frame
expectations.evidence: `water_shader = love.graphics.newShader([[...]])` inside `love.load()` â€” not in love.draw.

expectations.text: Uses `shader:hasUniform()` check before calling `shader:send()` to avoid errors on unused or optimized-out uniforms
expectations.evidence: Multiple `if water_shader:hasUniform("noise_tex") then` checks before each `shader:send()` call.

expectations.text: Applies `love.graphics.setShader(shader)` before drawing and `love.graphics.setShader()` (no argument) after to reset to the default shader
expectations.evidence: `love.graphics.setShader(water_shader)` before draw, `love.graphics.setShader()` after â€” correct reset pattern.

expectations.evidence: `love.graphics.setDefaultFilter("nearest", "nearest")` at start of `love.load()` before any image loading.

expectations.evidence: `love.graphics.setShader(shader)` before draw, `love.graphics.setShader()` after â€” correct reset pattern.

expectations.evidence: `love.graphics.setDefaultFilter("nearest", "nearest")` at start of `love.load()` before image loading.

expectations.text: Implements the Object Pool pattern: pre-allocates a fixed array of bullet tables with active/inactive flags, and the spawn function reuses inactive bullets rather than creating new ones
expectations.evidence: Pre-allocates `for i = 1, POOL_SIZE do bullets[i] = { x=0, y=0, vx=0, vy=0, alive=false } end` and spawn function checks `if not bullets[i].alive then` to reuse slots.

expectations.text: Applies the Flyweight pattern: loads the bullet image once in `love.load` and shares a single image reference across all bullet instances â€” never calls `love.graphics.newImage` per bullet
expectations.evidence: `bullet_image = love.graphics.newImage("assets/bullet.png")` called once in `love.load()`; all bullets reference the same image.

expectations.text: Never calls `love.graphics.newImage` (or any resource-loading function) inside `love.draw` or the spawn function â€” loading resources per frame is the explicit pitfall the skill warns against
expectations.evidence: `love.graphics.newImage` only called once in `love.load()`; not present in `love.draw` or `spawn_bullet`.

expectations.text: Multiplies movement by `dt` in `love.update`: `bullet.x = bullet.x + bullet.vx * dt` â€” frame-rate-independent movement is required
expectations.evidence: `b.x = b.x + b.vx * dt` and `b.y = b.y + b.vy * dt` â€” correct dt multiplication.

expectations.text: Draw loop iterates only over active bullets and uses `love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1, 1)` reset after any colored drawing to prevent tint bleed
expectations.evidence: `if b.alive then` check in draw loop; `love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1, 1)` reset before the draw loop.

expectations.evidence: No `love.graphics.push()` or `love.graphics.pop()` calls in the draw loop; transforms not isolated per button.
